nts1.d: Radio Jiro is the two hour show of artist Jiro Bevis, every 4 weeks, Monday 2-4pm. The show features a wide variety of sounds from across the globe with previous specials covering themes such as Meditation, Italian, UK punk, Japanese Techno-Pop, Library and French.
Parisian party instigator Louise Chen pulls from a timeless well of classic & modern house, disco, soul, pop, & more.
Hip-hop and soul-inflected sounds from Richmond's Ohbliv.
The snack enthusiast and London based producer and DJ takes on the NTS airwaves. Stay tuned for mixes spanning the breadth of dance and club music.

All the biggest bangers from around the world with Mina and guests every month.
From the host: "This month I've got lots of new and unreleased music from myself + Styn, Bryte, Roska, L-Vis 1990 & Surreal Sessions. Plus a guestmix from Maganda Shakul, a producer and percussionist from Uganda, who is pioneering his own Bakisimbatronics sound."

Record maker, player and mixer Martelo - not Martello - joins the musical dots with sonic heat from across the globe. Taking us to hip-hop, RnB, electronica and elsewhere, Martelo keeps it tight and keeps the heat: non-stop classics.

EndgamE’s Precious Metals lines up the latest in the sounds of the underbelly: drill, auto-tune serenades and dancehall. 808 snares rattle like uzis, deep synths sing out like a foghorn through the spray – both grime and hip-hop, tunes for the club and tunes for the car. Lock in. therealendgame@gmail.com

Monthly inter-dimensional techno and rhythm transmissions from GiGi FM.
From the host: "This month GiGi FM welcomes Shoal. A full production ambient journey through motion, growth and tension from the Dutch sound artist and ISOTOOP core member ~ sculptural sound design and inner movement for Aries season’s shifting skies."

nts1.n: A LONG STRANGE DREAM W/ BATU
nts1.d:
A Long Strange Dream is Batu's latest series which allows him to showcase a selection of music not limited to peak time dancefloors. Expect a deep dive into the crates for an eclectic mix of influences and oddities as you transcend into a collective long strange dream.
From the host: "loads of new tracks this month! shouts to everyone sending me dubs!"

Uruguay DJ, producer and sound designer Lechuga Zafiro presents “La Mano” (The Hand). It is an ode to the creative hands of the world. Hands that translate ideas and modify sound: hit the drum, touch the knobs, excite the strings. “La Mano” is always recorded live on CDJS and DJMs. Five fingers for rhythm, texture, weirdness, acoustic phenomena and sabor. Five fingers for each stroke of “La Clave”.
From the host: "La Mano comes back to club music. Heavy intervention on the 3 decks and the mixer. Sabrosa dance music with a pinch of weirdness. Unreleased stuff and upcoming releases from friends + some not so oldies."

nts1.n: Jupiter Presents: The Saturday Man by Aaliyah Bilal w/ Angel Dat Dawid & D-Composed
nts1.d: A story Aaliyah Bilal and a score by Angel Bat Dawid & D-Composed in response to 'And to Us is the return' (2025) by Armina Mussa Howada (Featured D-Composed Ensemble Members: Caitlin Edwards (Violin), Khelsey Zarraga (Violin), Seth Pae (Viola), Tahirah Whittington (Cello), Recording: The Land School, Recording & Post-Production Engineer: Dave Vettraino).

nts1.n: Jupiter Presents: they'll talk about us plenty by Tembe Denton-Hurst w/ Ivy Sole
Jupiter Presents: Ballistics by Briana Pickens w/ BSA Gold
THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ PAM
THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
RADIO SUPER W/ ROSA PISTOLA - Tributo a EROS

nts2.d: Bimonthly radio hour taking you down various musical rabbit holes.

High Jazz* is an independent record label specializing in 70s & 80s soul, jazz & funk. ill Dubio takes the reigns for the monthly High Jazz* show rustling up an hour of choice selections on a Wednesday.
From the host: "Harvest for the World"

All-genre NTS monthly by New York–based, New Delhi–born Arushi Jain fka modularprincess
From the host: "I recorded this DJ set live at Nowadays in NYC for the celebration of Nowruz, the ancient Persian New Year. I explored Persian classical strings, devotional voices (Hossein Alizadeh, Mahsa Vahdat, Hossein Omoumi), Armenian duduk, Indian raga, and Egyptian song with club-adjacent texture & bass."
London & Istanbul based record label Zel Zele takes us on a monthly ride to earthly wonders and cosmic adventures. Bite the lip take the trip!

Rio Doce, a deep dive into Brazilian music and its roots, with contributions from Caio Rosa and guests.
From the host: "Episode produced in the interior of Chapada Diamantina, a sacred land within the state of Bahia. A deep immersion in rivers rich in iron and crystals."

nts2.n: Amarillo on Everything w/ Hayden Pedigo
nts2.d: Guitarist and Amarillo native Hayden Pedigo takes you on a roadtrip through the Texas panhandle, sharing prog, country, and instrumental guitar favorites.

nts2.n: SÉANCE CENTRE w/ Fantasy in Orbit
nts2.d:
Canadian record label, publisher, and distributors Séance Centre conjure timeless music from the past, present, and future.
From the host: "This mix, from our friend Alex Yiaxis, aka Fantasy In Orbit is helping to bring much-needed warmth and sunshine. Formative club experiences and spending time behind the counter at Reckless Records in London opened up other dimensions for Alex, and since then he’s pursued sonic landscapes…"

New York based artist and musician Rahill selects a monthly medley of picks from her record collection, rooted in US jazz, with soul, folk and international sounds.
From the host: "the people united will never be defeated

nts2.n: No ID Radio: 100% Unreleased w/ Anysia Kym
nts2.d:
NYC-based producer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist Anysia Kym shares a monthly compilation of her favourite tracks, right now and of all time; she flips a song of her choice at the start of each hour-long episode.
From the host: "As a way to celebrate the return of NO ID RADIO, this episode is made up entirely of submitted unreleased tracks from listeners of the show, as well as unreleased tracks from my guest picks: duendita and DORIS (Frank Dorrey). Thanks to everyone who submitted, and for trusting me with your music."

nts2.n: La Cosecha Internacional: La Sonora Malandrina En Vivo Parte 2
nts2.d:
Inspired by Quinceñera parties in the hills of Mexico and beyond & rooted in partying as a form of immigrant resistance. La Cosecha was started by XOLO. Playing all Spanish, all vinyl norteñias, corridos, rancheras, cumbia and banda classics. Xolo's aim is to bring this music to a new generation.
From the host: "LA COSECHA INTERNACIONAL Y SU PRIMO SONIDO ALGO LEVE LLEGAN CON PURO FUEGO DIRECTO DE LA SAGRADA SANCHEZ TABOADA A LA IMAGINACION DE TODA LA GENTE ATRAVEZ DEL MUNDO."

nts2.n: Rambo Radio w/ DJ B.Cause & Mista B
nts2.d:
West Coast Rap tape, cd, and vinyl archives/mixes spanning the mid 80's to early 00's, from San Diego, L.A., Central Valley all the way up to the Bay Area, Sacramento, Northwest and Alaska! Artwork by: DJ TRIGG
From the host: "Special Guest Mix from DJ B.Cause & Mista B featuring Bay Area Tape classics!"

nts2.n: The Uline Catalog w/ Calvin LeCompte (R)
Temporal Cove w/ smilegoth (ari) and Forma Norte
Geologist Presents: The O'Brien System w/ Crown Vic

subtle.d: Hailing from northern Italy, STANDARD is a collective of artists, DJs and producers that aims at the promotion of bass music: Garage, UK Funky, Breaks, Leftfield Bass, Leftfield Techno, Dubstep, everything in between and beyond. They focus on the Italian Bass scene, showcasing new up-and-coming artists as well as established talents from all over the peninsula.

Oddkut is a producer & DJ hailing from Balakovo City, Russia. He's part of the Russian collective "United Gang" who are a Moscow-based community of local musicians, DJ's and promoters teamed up by their genuine love & interest in UK underground music, "TAЭT VREMYA" art group and the creator of the Moscow parties "Judder".

Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

dublab.n: Jennifer Herrema and Kurt Midness - The Banana Question

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: George Jensen and Heavy Hymns - Quietudes
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
Peak Oil records presents ‘tipping point’; a monthly journey through the record collections of brian and brion. Leaving no good genre unturned, enjoy two hours of esoterica, conversation and guests.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Greg Belson - Divine Chord Gospel Show
In Sheep's Clothing – World of Sound

rinsefr.j: : Music For The Soul #16 (Better Days w/ Matteo)
